# Data Integrity Checklist: Retro-migration - Version Marker and State-Keyed Backfill/Relocate

**Purpose**: Validate the quality of PRSG-011 data-integrity requirements for migration classification, non-mutating Tier-0 behavior, Tier-2 PROCESS relocation, CONTRACT protection, ID normalization, evidence normalization, and legacy review-packet recognition.
**Created**: 2026-06-09
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Tier-0 Historical Spec Protection

- [x] CHK001 - Does the spec require Tier-0 navigation backfill without stamping, moving, renaming, or otherwise mutating completed historical spec files outside generated navigation zones? [Completeness, Spec §FR-008, §FR-023]
- [x] CHK002 - Are requirements clear that Tier-0 is enabled only by the repo-level `.specify/structure-version.json` marker and delegated to `generate-spec-index.sh`, not a one-time direct INDEX patcher? [Consistency, Spec §FR-023, §FR-024; Plan §Increment 1]
- [x] CHK003 - Are marker-absent, malformed, unreadable, or below-v1 repo states specified so legacy-row discovery remains disabled and pre-marker behavior is preserved? [Edge Case, Spec §FR-023, Edge Cases]

## Tier-2 PROCESS and CONTRACT Classification

- [x] CHK004 - Is the Tier-2 PROCESS allow-list explicit enough to prevent moving files by broad glob or unbounded heuristic? [Completeness, Spec §FR-013; Contract §PROCESS Allow-List]
- [x] CHK005 - Are CONTRACT artifacts enumerated and protected even when their names resemble PROCESS artifacts? [Completeness, Spec §FR-014; Contract §CONTRACT Protections]
- [x] CHK006 - Are PROCESS-like filenames under `contracts/` and `checklists/` specified as protected CONTRACT paths where explicit protections win over allow-list matches? [Consistency, Spec §FR-014, Edge Cases]
- [x] CHK007 - Are docs-side PROCESS moves limited to `<SPEC-ID>-design-concept.md` and `<SPEC-ID>-workflow.md` that match the thawed spec ID, while unrelated docs, roadmaps, PRDs, and existing `.process/` files stay in place? [Coverage, Spec §FR-025]

## ID Normalization and Frozen-State Joins

- [x] CHK008 - Does PRSG-011 require reusing `moc_normalize` and `moc_id_match` instead of introducing a duplicate ID grammar? [Consistency, Spec §Assumptions; Plan §Constitution Check; Research §Decision 2]
- [x] CHK009 - Are false joins such as `PRSG-013A` versus `PRSG-013A1` addressed through exact opaque segment matching and planned fixture coverage? [Traceability, Research §Decision 2; Plan §Increment 1; PRSG-002 ID grammar]
- [x] CHK010 - Is `.specify/feature.json` parsing specified as tri-state and used as the authoritative frozen/in-flight skip source for every migration tier? [Completeness, Spec §FR-009; Data Model §ActiveFeatureState]

## Evidence Normalization

- [x] CHK011 - Does the spec consistently define the canonical final target for legacy evidence as a `.process`-anchored path, given that `evidence/` and `verification-evidence.md` are PROCESS artifacts but some artifacts currently describe normalization only as `evidence/verification-evidence.md`? [Resolved, Consistency, Spec §FR-013, §FR-015, §FR-025; Contract §Evidence Normalization] - Canonical target is now `<spec-dir>/.process/evidence/verification-evidence.md`.
- [x] CHK012 - Are collision and no-op rules complete for competing evidence sources, including root `verification-evidence.md`, root `evidence/verification-evidence.md`, and already-normalized `.process/evidence/verification-evidence.md`? [Resolved, Edge Case, Spec §FR-015; Contract §Evidence Normalization] - Competing source and existing-target cases now report collision and apply fails before mutation.

## Legacy Review Packet Recognition

- [x] CHK013 - Does the spec resolve the legacy `peer-review-*` alias versus canonical `pr-review-packet.md` target, including whether legacy basenames are preserved or canonicalized during Tier-2 relocation? [Resolved, Clarity, Spec §FR-013, Edge Cases; PRSG-011 Design Concept Q2] - Legacy aliases now canonicalize to `<spec-dir>/.process/pr-review-packet.md`; legacy basenames are not preserved.
- [x] CHK014 - Are collision and no-overwrite rules defined when `pr-review-packet.md`, one or more legacy `peer-review-*` files, or an existing `.process/pr-review-packet.md` target compete for the same canonical review-packet destination? [Resolved, Edge Case, Spec §FR-013; Contract §Review Packet Canonicalization] - Competing review-packet sources and existing canonical target conflicts now report collision and fail before mutation.

## Reports, Idempotency, and Measurable Outcomes

- [x] CHK015 - Does operator-facing JSON distinguish pending, applied, no-op, protected CONTRACT, collision, frozen/in-flight, dirty-tree blocked, backup, and recovery decisions? [Completeness, Spec §FR-021; Contract §Migration Report JSON]
- [x] CHK016 - Are idempotency requirements defined for both Tier-1/Tier-0 and Tier-2 re-runs after successful migration? [Measurability, Spec §FR-006, §FR-018; SC-007]
- [x] CHK017 - Do success criteria quantify that 0 CONTRACT or unrelated docs artifacts move and 100% of allowed PROCESS artifacts move to the correct anchor? [Measurability, Spec §SC-004]
- [x] CHK018 - Are scaffold/autopilot requirements clear that Tier-2 detection is static and never auto-executes dry-run or apply, protecting migration data from surprise codemods? [Scenario Coverage, Spec §FR-020, §FR-026]

## Rerun Verification

- [x] CHK019 - Is the canonical legacy evidence target now consistently `.process`-anchored across spec, data model, CLI contract, plan, and quickstart? [Spec §FR-015; Data Model §MoveOperation; Contract §Evidence Normalization; Plan §Increment 2; Quickstart §Tier-2 Relocation Apply]
- [x] CHK020 - Are competing evidence source and existing-target cases now required to report collision and fail before mutation without overwriting unrelated evidence? [Spec §FR-015; Contract §Evidence Normalization]
- [x] CHK021 - Is `pr-review-packet.md` now the canonical relocated review-packet target while legacy `peer-review-*` is recognized as an input alias? [Spec §FR-013; Contract §Review Packet Canonicalization]
- [x] CHK022 - Are competing review-packet source and existing-target cases now required to report collision and fail before mutation? [Spec §FR-013; Contract §Review Packet Canonicalization]

## Notes

- Initial data-integrity run found four gap markers across two remediation areas: evidence canonical target/collision semantics and legacy review-packet canonicalization/collision semantics. All four were resolved by tightening `spec.md`, `data-model.md`, `contracts/relocate-process-artifacts-cli.md`, `plan.md`, and `quickstart.md`.
- Rerun verification added CHK019-CHK022 and found no new gaps.
